threats.title: JWT Token in localStorage — XSS Token Theft
threats.scenario: frontend/src/app/Services/request.interceptor.ts:13 stores JWT in localStorage. Any XSS payload can call localStorage.getItem('token') and exfiltrate to attacker server. Token grants 6h session.

steps: Move JWT to HttpOnly Secure SameSite=Strict cookie
Implement BFF pattern to avoid SPA holding raw JWT

threats.title: Stored XSS via bypassSecurityTrustHtml in About and Administration Components
threats.scenario: about.component.ts:119 calls sanitizer.bypassSecurityTrustHtml(feedback.comment) rendering attacker-controlled HTML. Admin who views /about executes payload — token exfiltration via T-005.
threats.controls_in_place: Angular DomSanitizer present but explicitly bypassed

steps: Sanitize HTML server-side with sanitize-html before storing

threats.title: OAuth Access Token Passed as URL Query Parameter
threats.scenario: frontend/src/app/Services/user.service.ts:68 calls Google userinfo with access_token in URL. Token leaks to Express access log (/support/logs publicly readable), Referer headers, and proxy logs.

steps: Move access_token to Authorization: Bearer header in HTTP request

threats.title: No Content Security Policy — XSS Impact Amplification
threats.scenario: No CSP header set (helmet.contentSecurityPolicy() not called; xssFilter commented out at server.ts:187). Any XSS can load external scripts, exfiltrate data, and perform CSRF without restriction.

steps: Enable helmet.contentSecurityPolicy() with strict script-src directive
Use nonce or hash for any inline scripts
